Rodrigo Nascimento Hernandez
rodrigo****@gmail.com - https://x.com/rodrigonh2 - +********
March 2026
Age: 49
Origin/location: Brazil, holds Spanish (Spain) citizenship
Hands-on experience: Hands-on technologist with 15+ years bridging hardware,
software, and systems. Built and deployed production systems spanning UAV,
geoprocessing pipelines, and server infrastructure. Track record of delivering
end-to-end solutions: concept → prototype → production → maintenance. Experience
in field operations. Visiting professor at Pontifical Catholic Universities (PUC
Minas).
Core competences:
• Full-stack development: Python, JavaScript/HTML, LUA
• Geoprocessing & photogrammetry: GDAL, spatial analysis, land classification
• UAV systems: Custom builds, flight control, navigation software, field
operations
• Electronics: PCB design (KiCad/DipTrace), power systems, RF/antennas (Ham
operator PU4RNH)
• Systems administration: FreeBSD, nginx, ZFS, email infrastructure
Drone experience:
Custom EdgeTX navigation system (including map, bearing, go-to functionality,
among others), for ArduPilot CRSF passthru protocol, programmed in LUA. Can be
checked here https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ua8J6P4wYl0 and the code can be
fetched here https://github.com/Rodrigo-NH/SmallTelemetryScript . Even though
the repository is a fork, it’s a complete rewrite. Proceed as a fork for ethical
reasons, considering I used information contained in Yaapu’s code for the
ArduPilot CRSF passthru protocol.
Experience by developing a drone project. Project is a customized drone solution
developed from raw modules (IMU, frame, motors etc) and companion parts
(protection, gimbal, retractable landing gears, antenna holders etc) projected
and 3D printed by myself.
Programming experience:
• Ardupilot Mission Planner integration:
Development of a terrain following mission generator for Ardupilot/Mission
Planner. For security guardrails (anti-collision) (do not rely on
Ardupilot’s embedded terrain system but defined pre-flight). This mission
generator takes KML GoogleEarth archives as input, for easy mission creation
with altitude guardrails. The system can be used and evaluated here:
https://rodrigo-nh.github.io/TerraclipTestPage/ and the code can be viewed
here https://github.com/Rodrigo-NH/Terraclip
• Topographic (angles) and EDM (distance) file generator for closed polygons:
This page relies on python and GDAL backend to produce specific topographic
documents required by Brazilian’s bureaucracy. Can be used here:
https://www.descritivo.com.br/
Able to integrate technologies in geoprocessing as general, example
https://github.com/Rodrigo-NH/UE4HmapsGenerator
• Able to participate in a professional shape and code along Q/A requirements.
Example: https://github.com/BastilleBSD/bastille/pull/660
I’m a seasoned programmer, though not selling programs or programming services.
I hold strong geoprocessing knowledge, this specific knowledge used
professionally, almost daily, in aerial photogrammetry, land use classification,
vector creation/manipulation, among other tasks. Able to deal with geoprocessing
programmatically (python + GDAL + JavaScript + HTML).

Electronics:
• End-to-end PCB development: schematic → layout → in-house fabrication (dry
film photolithography or CNC plotting)
• Power systems, motor control, noise mitigation
• Component sourcing, datasheet analysis, reference design implementation
• RF transmission lines and antenna design (VHF/UHF)
Experience in diagnosis and problem-solving in electronics (from basic to
moderate complexity), including power systems, motors, noise control. Use of
DipTrace/Kicad for PCB prototyping and inhouse PCB fabrication (DryFilm or CNC
plotting). Experience using LTspice for prototyping and solutions (from basic to
moderate complexity). Experience in sourcing parts (Texas Instruments, others),
reading datasheets, study of white papers and reference designs. Ham operator
(PU4RNH) with experience in RF transmission lines and antennas (mainly VHF/UHF).
Example projects from (from circuit diagram to PCB to fabrication):
There is the current scope for professional development, the sense of purpose,
and the opportunity to work in a multitasking environment. Furthermore, I am an
enthusiast of military gear. Moreover, considering the geopolitical landscape
and the undeniable importance of the United States, all signs indicate that we
are living through historic times. Participating in projects within
this context aligns directly with my vision of how the United States should act:
by safeguarding freedom and Western values.
